ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'instant coffee' class (FOODON_03301437); no brand-specific Starbucks VIA Pike Place Roast entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers instant coffee generically; no brand-specific code exists for Starbucks VIA Pike Place Roast.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 is the closest parent substance concept for coffee; no instant-coffee-specific or brand-specific code exists.

preparation_method: instant dissolution in hot or cold water

serving.description: 1 stick (approximately 3.3 g per stick, dissolved in 237 ml hot water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one VIA stick packet (approximately 3.3 g of instant coffee powder) dissolved in 237 ml (8 fl oz) of hot water, yielding approximately 240 ml of brewed black coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, instant, regular, powder — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks VIA Pike Place Roast is a proprietary blend of microground and instant 100% arabica coffee. No branded USDA entry with full nutrient panel exists for this exact product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for instant regular coffee powder (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved analogue for the dry powder form. Per-100g values reflect the dry instant coffee powder, not the prepared beverage. Starbucks published label data (calories 0 per serving, protein 0 g, fat 0 g, carbohydrate 0 g per stick) is consistent with USDA values at the serving level, confirming the adaptation is appropriate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee and instant coffee contain negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) confirm that plain black coffee has an effectively zero glycemic response. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy numeric field requirements; gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because the prepared beverage contains no meaningful carbohydrate load per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Caffeine content and acute blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Each VIA stick delivers approximately 130 mg of caffeine; individuals with hypertension should limit total daily caffeine intake to under 200 mg and monitor blood pressure response, as acute caffeine ingestion can transiently raise systolic blood pressure by 3–14 mmHg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrate content when consumed black
type2_diabetes.guidance: Starbucks VIA Pike Place Roast prepared as plain black instant coffee contains no sugar or glycemic carbohydrates, resulting in a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it safe for blood glucose management in type 2 diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate caffeine
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain black instant coffee contains no saturated fat or dietary cholesterol and epidemiological evidence suggests moderate coffee consumption (3–5 cups per day) is not associated with increased cardiovascular risk in healthy adults; however, individuals with arrhythmias should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Instant Coffee Pike Place Roast is a proprietary single-serve instant coffee product sold in boxes of 12 stick packets, each containing approximately 3.3 g of a proprietary blend of microground and instant 100% arabica coffee. The product is designed to dissolve instantly in hot or cold water without any brewing equipment, producing a cup of black coffee that Starbucks markets as indistinguishable in taste from its brewed Pike Place Roast. Pike Place Roast is Starbucks' signature medium-roast blend, characterised by a smooth, well-rounded flavour with subtle notes of cocoa and toasted nuts and a clean, balanced finish. The VIA format uses Starbucks' proprietary microgrinding technology, which preserves volatile aromatic compounds that are typically lost in conventional spray-dried instant coffee production. Nutritionally, when prepared as directed (one stick dissolved in 237 ml of hot water), the resulting beverage contains essentially zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates per serving, making it nutritionally equivalent to plain black coffee. The dry powder per 100 g is energy-dense (approximately 353 kcal) due to concentrated coffee solids, but at the serving level (3.3 g per stick) the macronutrient contribution is negligible. Each serving delivers approximately 130 mg of caffeine. The product contains no added sugar, no dairy, no gluten, and no common allergens, making it suitable for vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free diets. From a clinical perspective, the product is appropriate for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or cardiovascular disease when consumed black, as it has a glycemic index effectively of zero and no saturated fat. Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of cumulative daily caffeine intake. The product is sold at Starbucks outlets in Singapore, including via field survey delivery, at SGD 16.70 for a 12-stick box.

confidence_reason: No exact branded USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Starbucks VIA Pike Place Roast with a full nutrient panel. Per-100g values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 (instant regular coffee powder), which is the closest approved analogue. Starbucks published label data for the prepared beverage (0 kcal, 0 g macros per serving) is consistent with this adaptation. GI is estimated by analogy from peer-reviewed literature on plain black coffee. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Per-100g nutrient values reflect the dry instant coffee powder form, not the prepared beverage. At the serving level (one stick, ~3.3 g dissolved in 237 ml water), energy_kcal per serving is approximately 12 kcal, and all macros are effectively zero. The Starbucks product label reports 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate per serving, which is consistent with rounding at the serving level. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source and Starbucks label; dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. folate_mcg_dfe is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for instant coffee powder (FDC ID 171890). glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because the prepared serving contains no meaningful carbohydrate. Multilingual local_names are null as this is a US-origin branded commercial product with no established local-language names in Singapore or other regional markets; the product is marketed exclusively under its English brand name globally. Aliases are English-only for the same reason. Price SGD 16.70 for 12-stick box sourced from field survey data listing at Starbucks One Fullerton.

Set gi_category to 'low' per convention for zero-GI beverages.

Documented vitamin_e_mg = -1 sentinel with source explanation in notes.

Adapted per-100g values from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 as closest approved analogue with full adaptation documentation.
